{"description": "Design inventory implementation architecture", "subagent_type": "Plan", "prompt": "Design the complete implementation plan for the HarborOps inventory module expansion. I'll provide you all the context you need.

## Repository Context

Location: `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/HarborOps/TASK-87/repo`

Stack: Express + TypeScript + Prisma ORM + MySQL 8, Vue 3 frontend, Docker Compose

## Current State

### Files that exist:
- `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.routes.ts` - Has basic CRUD inline (no service/controller split). Contains: GET/POST /items, GET /vendors, POST /vendors, GET /low-stock, GET /ledger
- `backend/src/lib/cache.ts` - TTLCache singleton with get/set/delete/clear/has/cleanup
- `backend/src/lib/exporter.ts` - exportToCsv(data, columns, options) and exportToExcel(data, columns, sheetName, options)
- `backend/src/lib/scheduler.ts` - registerJob/startScheduler, has placeholder nightly KPI job
- `backend/src/middleware/auth.ts` - authenticate() and authorize(...roles) middleware
- `backend/src/types/index.ts` - successResponse, errorResponse, paginate, AuthenticatedRequest, ApiResponse
- `backend/src/config/index.ts` - inventory.varianceApprovalPct=5, inventory.varianceApprovalUsd=250, inventory.defaultSafetyThreshold=10, inventory.defaultAvgUsageDays=7

### Prisma Models (all already in schema - NO schema changes needed):
- **Item**: id, name, sku, category, description, isLotControlled, unitOfMeasure, unitPrice, isActive
- **Vendor**: id, name, contactEncrypted, isActive
- **Location**: id, name, address, isActive
- **Lot**: id, itemId, lotNumber, expirationDate — @@unique([itemId, lotNumber])
- **StockLevel**: id, itemId, locationId, lotId?, onHand, safetyThreshold, avgDailyUsage — @@unique([itemId, locationId, lotId])
- **InventoryLedger**: id, itemId, lotId?, fromLocationId?, toLocationId?, movementType (RECEIVING/ISSUE/TRANSFER/STOCK_COUNT/ADJUSTMENT), quantity, unitCostUsd?, vendorId?, packSize?, deliveryDatetime?, referenceNumber, performedBy, approvedBy?, notes, createdAt
- **StockCount**: id, locationId, initiatedBy, status (DRAFT/PENDING_APPROVAL/APPROVED/REJECTED), variancePct?, varianceUsd?, approvedBy?, createdAt, completedAt?
- **StockCountLine**: id, stockCountId, itemId, lotId?, systemQty, countedQty, varianceQty, variancePct?, varianceUsd?
- **KpiDaily**: id, date (unique), dau, conversionRate, aov, repurchaseRate, refundRate

### Auth pattern (existing in auth module):
- auth.schema.ts - Zod schemas
- auth.service.ts - Business logic class
- auth.controller.ts - Thin Express handlers
- auth.routes.ts - Router that wires middleware + controller methods

## Requirements

Design the implementation plan covering:

### 1. File structure
- `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.schema.ts` - All Zod validators
- `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.service.ts` - Business logic
- `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.controller.ts` - Thin HTTP handlers
- Replace inline logic in `inventory.routes.ts`
- Update `backend/src/lib/scheduler.ts` for real KPI job
- `unit_tests/inventory.test.ts` - Unit tests
- `API_tests/inventory.test.ts` - API tests

### 2. Endpoints to implement

**Master data (enhance existing + add new):**
- GET /items (enhance: add search, active filter)
- POST /items (exists)
- GET /items/:id (exists)
- PATCH /items/:id (new: update item)
- GET /items/categories (new: distinct categories list)
- GET /vendors (exists)
- POST /vendors (exists, needs contact encryption)
- PATCH /vendors/:id (new)
- GET /lots?itemId=X (new: list lots for item)
- PATCH /stock-levels/:id/threshold (new: update safety threshold)

**Movement workflows:**
- POST /receive — Body: {vendorId, itemId, locationId, quantity, unitCostUsd, packSize, deliveryDatetime, lotNumber?, expirationDate?, notes?}
  - If isLotControlled: require lotNumber
  - Upsert lot, find-or-create stock level, increment onHand, create RECEIVING ledger entry
  
- POST /issue — Body: {itemId, locationId, quantity, lotId?, notes?}
  - If isLotControlled: require lotId
  - Check onHand >= quantity (error if not)
  - Decrement stock level, create ISSUE ledger entry
  
- POST /transfer — Body: {itemId, fromLocationId, toLocationId, quantity, lotId?, notes?}
  - If isLotControlled: require lotId
  - MUST verify source onHand >= quantity (never allow negative)
  - Use Prisma $transaction: decrement source, find-or-create + increment dest, create TRANSFER ledger entry
  - Single ledger entry with both fromLocationId and toLocationId

**Stock counts:**
- GET /stock-counts — list with filters (locationId, status)
- POST /stock-counts — Body: {locationId}
  - Fetch all StockLevels for that location
  - Create StockCount (DRAFT) + StockCountLines with systemQty=currentOnHand, countedQty=systemQty, varianceQty=0
  
- PUT /stock-counts/:id/lines — Body: {lines: [{lineId, countedQty}]}
  - Only works when status=DRAFT
  - Recalculate varianceQty, variancePct, varianceUsd per line
  
- POST /stock-counts/:id/finalize
  - Only when status=DRAFT
  - Aggregate: totalSystemQty, totalVarianceQty (absolute), totalVarianceUsd
  - variancePct = abs(totalVarianceQty) / totalSystemQty * 100
  - varianceUsd = sum of absolute(varianceUsd per line)
  - If variancePct > 5 OR varianceUsd > 250: set status=PENDING_APPROVAL
  - Else: auto-approve (apply adjustments, set status=APPROVED, set completedAt)
  
- POST /stock-counts/:id/approve — MANAGER, ADMIN only
  - Only when status=PENDING_APPROVAL
  - Apply adjustments: for each line where varianceQty != 0, update StockLevel.onHand and create STOCK_COUNT ledger entry
  - Set status=APPROVED, completedAt=now(), approvedBy=req.user.userId

- POST /stock-counts/:id/reject — MANAGER, ADMIN only
  - Only when status=PENDING_APPROVAL
  - Set status=REJECTED (no stock level changes)

**Low-stock:**
- GET /low-stock (enhance existing)
  - Add locationId filter
  - Add item details in response
  - Threshold = max(safetyThreshold, avgDailyUsage * 7)

**Ledger:**
- GET /ledger (enhance existing)
  - Add sort parameter (field, direction)
  - Return column metadata for frontend
  - Keep all existing filters

- GET /ledger/export?format=csv|excel&...filters...
  - Re-uses same filter logic as ledger
  - Non-ADMIN users: set unitCostUsd to null in export
  - Uses exportToCsv / exportToExcel from lib/exporter.ts
  - Streams response with proper Content-Type headers

**Stock levels:**
- GET /stock-levels?itemId=&locationId= — filtered stock level list

### 3. KPI aggregation job (nightly at 2 AM)

Replace scheduler.ts placeholder with real implementation:
- Count distinct users who created RateLimitLog entries yesterday (approximate DAU)
- Calculate inventory turnover: total ISSUE quantity / avg stock level
- Upsert KpiDaily record for yesterday's date
- Update avgDailyUsage on StockLevels: for each (itemId, locationId, lotId) combo, count ISSUE quantities over last 30 days, divide by 30

Also register a new job: nightly-avg-usage-update (0 3 * * *) — updates avgDailyUsage for all stock levels

### 4. RBAC enforcement
- GET /items, /vendors, /low-stock, /stock-levels, /lots: authenticate only (all roles)
- POST/PATCH /items: ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK
- POST/PATCH /vendors: ADMIN, MANAGER
- POST /receive, /issue, /transfer: ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK
- GET/POST /stock-counts, PUT /stock-counts/:id/lines, POST /stock-counts/:id/finalize: ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK
- POST /stock-counts/:id/approve, reject: ADMIN, MANAGER
- GET /ledger, /ledger/export: MANAGER, ADMIN
- PATCH /stock-levels/:id/threshold: ADMIN, MANAGER
- Vendor costs (contactEncrypted, unitCostUsd): only shown to ADMIN

### 5. Tests

Unit tests (in unit_tests/inventory.test.ts):
- Test variance calculation logic (mock data, test threshold determination)
- Test low-stock threshold formula (max of safetyThreshold vs avgDailyUsage * 7)
- Test that transfer validation correctly rejects when quantity > onHand

API tests (in API_tests/inventory.test.ts):
- Happy path: receive → check stock levels → issue → verify decrement → transfer → verify source/dest
- RBAC: guest cannot receive, issue, or transfer
- Stock count: create → submit lines → finalize (both auto-approve and pending paths)
- Vendor cost visibility: non-admin cannot see unitCostUsd in ledger export

### 6. Key technical decisions

- Use Prisma $transaction for transfer and stock-count application (atomicity)
- referenceNumber on ledger: format as `{TYPE}-{YYYYMMDD}-{random4}` (e.g., "RCV-20240301-A1B2")
- For finding/creating stock level in receive: use prisma.stockLevel.upsert on [itemId, locationId, lotId]
- Export: respond with res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', 'attachment; filename="ledger.csv"')
- KPI DAU: count distinct userId from rate_limit_logs where createdAt >= yesterday midnight AND < today midnight

Please design the detailed implementation plan including the exact Zod schemas, service method signatures, controller method shapes, and route definitions. Make it specific enough to execute directly without ambiguity."}

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/.claude/plans/zany-petting-galaxy.md", "content": "# HarborOps Inventory Module — Full Implementation Plan

## Context

The HarborOps foundation scaffold is complete and boots with `docker compose up`. The Prisma schema already contains all 35 models (no schema/migration changes needed). The inventory module currently has basic CRUD inline in `inventory.routes.ts` with no services or controllers. This plan implements all inventory business logic, KPI cron jobs, exports, and tests end-to-end.

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.schema.ts`
All Zod validators organized by domain section. Key schemas:

- `createItemSchema` / `updateItemSchema` (partial of create)
- `createVendorSchema` / `updateVendorSchema` — accepts plaintext `contact` field; service encrypts it
- `updateThresholdSchema` — `{ safetyThreshold: z.number().int().min(0) }`
- `receiveSchema` — `{ vendorId, itemId, locationId, quantity, unitCostUsd?, packSize?, deliveryDatetime?, lotNumber?, expirationDate?, notes? }`
- `issueSchema` — `{ itemId, locationId, quantity, lotId?, notes? }`
- `transferSchema` — `{ itemId, fromLocationId, toLocationId, quantity, lotId?, notes? }`
- `createStockCountSchema` — `{ locationId }`
- `updateLinesSchema` — `{ lines: [{ lineId, countedQty }] }`
- `itemsQuerySchema` — adds `search` and `isActive` fields to existing pagination
- `stockCountQuerySchema` — `{ locationId?, status?, page?, pageSize? }`
- `ledgerQuerySchema` — all existing filters + `sortField`, `sortDir`, `format` (csv|excel)

### 2. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.service.ts`
Single class `InventoryService` exported as singleton `inventoryService`. No Express types — pure business logic.

**Private helpers:**
- `generateRefNum(type: 'RCV'|'ISS'|'TRF'|'STC'|'ADJ'): string` → `RCV-20240301-A1B2`
- `buildLedgerWhere(query)` — shared filter builder for ledger and export (DRY)
- `applyStockCountAdjustments(tx, stockCountId, lines, approvedBy)` — shared by finalize (auto-approve) and approve

**Master data methods:**
- `listItems(query)` — paginated, supports search (name/sku/category contains), isActive filter
- `createItem(input)` — direct create
- `getItem(id)` — include stockLevels+lots; throw 404 if missing
- `updateItem(id, input)` — partial update
- `listCategories()` — distinct category values from active items
- `listVendors(isAdmin)` — omit `contactEncrypted` for non-admin
- `createVendor(input)` — encrypt `contact` → `contactEncrypted` via `encrypt()`
- `updateVendor(id, input)` — encrypt contact if provided
- `listLots(itemId)` — filter by itemId
- `listStockLevels(itemId?, locationId?)` — filtered, include item/location/lot
- `updateThreshold(id, input)` — update safetyThreshold on StockLevel

**Movement methods:**
- `receive(input, performedBy)`:
  1. Fetch item → check `isLotControlled`; throw 422 `LOT_REQUIRED` if missing lotNumber
  2. Upsert lot via `@@unique([itemId, lotNumber])`
  3. Upsert StockLevel via `@@unique([itemId, locationId, lotId])` with `onHand: { increment: quantity }`
  4. Create `RECEIVING` ledger entry; `toLocationId = locationId`

- `issue(input, performedBy)`:
  1. Fetch item → isLotControlled check → throw 422 `LOT_REQUIRED`
  2. Find StockLevel; throw 404 if missing
  3. If `onHand < quantity` → throw 422 `INSUFFICIENT_STOCK`
  4. Decrement StockLevel
  5. Create `ISSUE` ledger entry; `fromLocationId = locationId`

- `transfer(input, performedBy)` — **must use `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})`**:
  1. Fetch item → isLotControlled check
  2. Inside tx: find source StockLevel; throw 404/422 as needed
  3. Decrement source; upsert dest (same pattern as receive)
  4. Create single `TRANSFER` ledger entry with both `fromLocationId` and `toLocationId`

**Stock count methods:**
- `createStockCount(locationId, initiatedBy)`:
  1. Fetch all StockLevels for locationId
  2. `prisma.$transaction`: create StockCount (DRAFT) + StockCountLines (systemQty=onHand, countedQty=onHand, varianceQty=0)

- `updateLines(stockCountId, input)`:
  1. Verify status === DRAFT; throw 422 `INVALID_STATE` otherwise
  2. For each line: recalculate varianceQty, variancePct (null if systemQty=0), varianceUsd (null if no unitPrice)

- `finalizeStockCount(stockCountId)`:
  1. Verify status === DRAFT
  2. Aggregate: `overallVariancePct = abs(totalVarianceQty) / totalSystemQty * 100`, `totalVarianceUsd = sum(abs(line.varianceUsd))`
  3. `needsApproval = overallVariancePct > 5 || totalVarianceUsd > 250`
  4. If needsApproval: set `PENDING_APPROVAL`; else: call `applyStockCountAdjustments` + set `APPROVED`, `completedAt`

- `approveStockCount(stockCountId, approvedBy)` — MANAGER/ADMIN: verify `PENDING_APPROVAL`, apply adjustments in tx, set APPROVED
- `rejectStockCount(stockCountId)` — set REJECTED (no ledger entries, no stock changes)

**`applyStockCountAdjustments(tx, stockCountId, lines, approvedBy)`:**
- For each line where `varianceQty !== 0`: update `StockLevel.onHand += varianceQty` + create `STOCK_COUNT` ledger entry

**Query/export methods:**
- `getLowStock(locationId?)` — filter `onHand < max(safetyThreshold, avgDailyUsage * 7)`, include item/location/lot, return with computed `threshold` field
- `getLedger(query, isAdmin)` — paginated, sorted, includes `columns` metadata array; mask `unitCostUsd = null` for non-admin; omit `unitCostUsd` from columns for non-admin
- `exportLedger(query, isAdmin, username)` — no pagination, mask costs for non-admin, call `exportToCsv`/`exportToExcel` from `lib/exporter.ts`, return `{ data, format, filename }`

### 3. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.controller.ts`
Class `InventoryController` exported as singleton `inventoryController`. Arrow-function methods. Pattern: parse → call service → `res.json(successResponse(...))`.

Business error handling in every method:
```typescript
} catch (err: any) {
  if (err.statusCode) {
    res.status(err.statusCode).json(errorResponse(err.code, err.message));
    return;
  }
  next(err);
}
```

`exportLedger` controller does NOT use `res.json` — instead sets `Content-Type` + `Content-Disposition` headers and calls `res.send(result.data)`.

`isAdmin` computed as `req.user!.role === 'ADMIN'` in each relevant method.

---

## Files to Update

### 4. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.routes.ts` (complete replacement)
Wire controller methods. Critical ordering: static routes before dynamic ones.

```
GET  /items             → listItems
POST /items             → createItem         (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /items/categories  → listCategories     ← BEFORE /items/:id
GET  /items/:id         → getItem
PATCH /items/:id        → updateItem         (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /vendors           → listVendors
POST /vendors           → createVendor       (ADMIN, MANAGER)
PATCH /vendors/:id      → updateVendor       (ADMIN, MANAGER)
GET  /lots              → listLots
GET  /stock-levels      → listStockLevels
PATCH /stock-levels/:id/threshold → updateThreshold (ADMIN, MANAGER)
POST /receive           → receive            (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /issue             → issue              (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /transfer          → transfer           (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /stock-counts      → listStockCounts    (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts      → createStockCount   (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
PUT  /stock-counts/:id/lines     → updateLines       (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts/:id/finalize  → finalizeStockCount(ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts/:id/approve   → approveStockCount (ADMIN, MANAGER)
POST /stock-counts/:id/reject    → rejectStockCount  (ADMIN, MANAGER)
GET  /low-stock         → getLowStock
GET  /ledger/export     → exportLedger       (MANAGER, ADMIN) ← BEFORE /ledger
GET  /ledger            → getLedger          (MANAGER, ADMIN)
```

### 5. `backend/src/lib/scheduler.ts`
Replace placeholder `nightly-kpi-aggregation` job with real implementation:
- Count distinct userId from `rateLimitLog` yesterday → `dau`
- Sum ISSUE quantities yesterday / avg onHand → `conversionRate` (inventory turnover proxy)
- `prisma.kpiDaily.upsert` by `date`

Add new `nightly-avg-usage-update` job at `0 3 * * *`:
- `prisma.inventoryLedger.groupBy(['itemId', 'fromLocationId', 'lotId'])` for last 30 days of ISSUE movements
- `avgDailyUsage = sum / 30`
- `prisma.stockLevel.updateMany(...)` per group

Use dynamic `import('./prisma')` pattern (already used by cache-cleanup job — do not add static imports at top).

---

## New Test Files

### 6. `unit_tests/inventory.test.ts`
Pure logic tests — no database, no HTTP. Inline the pure formulas extracted from service methods:
- **Variance calculation** (5 tests): zero variance auto-approves; >5% triggers approval; USD >$250 triggers approval even at low pct; both within bounds → auto-approve; null unitPrice contributes $0
- **Low-stock threshold** (4 tests): safetyThreshold wins when higher; avgDailyUsage*7 wins when higher; exact-match is not low-stock; zero avgDailyUsage falls back to safetyThreshold
- **Transfer validation** (4 tests): rejects when requested > onHand; allows when equal; allows when less; zero stock rejects

### 7. `API_tests/inventory.test.ts`
Integration tests using `api` and `loginAs`/`demoUsers` from `./helpers`. Module-level `let` vars for captured IDs.

- **Happy path** (6 tests): GET items → GET vendors → GET locations → POST receive → verify stock increment → POST issue → verify decrement → POST transfer → verify source/dest; also tests 422 for insufficient stock
- **RBAC** (6 tests): guest blocked from receive/issue/transfer; unauthenticated gets 401; clerk blocked from approve; non-admin cannot see contactEncrypted in vendor list
- **Stock count workflow** (4 tests): create DRAFT → update lines → finalize auto-approve (zero variance) → finalize PENDING_APPROVAL (high variance); MANAGER can approve PENDING_APPROVAL count
- **Ledger export & cost visibility** (4 tests): non-admin gets unitCostUsd=null + columns omits field; admin sees unitCostUsd; CSV export returns correct Content-Type + Content-Disposition; Excel export returns spreadsheetml content-type

---

## Key Technical Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `prisma.$transaction(async tx => {...})` for transfer | Need conditional read before write — array form can't do this |
| `updateMany` for avgDailyUsage in scheduler | `lotId` is nullable; Prisma unique update is unreliable with nullable composite keys |
| `lotId ?? null` in upsert where clauses | Prisma requires explicit `null`, not `undefined`, for nullable composite unique fields |
| Business errors as `Object.assign(new Error(...), { statusCode, code })` | Caught in controller before `next(err)`, returns clean JSON without hitting global handler |
| `GET /items/categories` declared before `GET /items/:id` | Express router matches first; `categories` would otherwise be treated as `:id` |
| `GET /ledger/export` declared before `GET /ledger` | Same route ordering issue |
| Lot-controlled validation in service (not schema) | Requires DB fetch to know `isLotControlled`; Zod schemas keep lotNumber/lotId optional |
| `applyStockCountAdjustments` private helper | Reused by both finalize (auto-approve) and approve — eliminates duplication |
| Nightly job uses dynamic `import('./prisma')` | Matches existing cache-cleanup pattern in scheduler.ts |
| Export does not paginate | Export fetches all matching records (remove skip/take) |

---

## Verification

After implementation, verify with the running stack (`docker compose up -d`):

```bash
# Login as clerk
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"clerk","password":"clerk123!"}' | jq -r .data.token)

# Receive stock
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/inventory/receive \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"vendorId":1,"itemId":1,"locationId":1,"quantity":50,"unitCostUsd":9.99}'

# Issue stock
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/inventory/issue \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"itemId":1,"locationId":1,"quantity":5}'

# Create and finalize stock count
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/inventory/stock-counts \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"locationId":1}'

# Run full test suite
./run_tests.sh
```

Expected: all unit tests pass, all API tests pass, no TypeScript compilation errors in Docker build.
"}

- `ledgerQuerySchema` — all existing filters + `sortField`, `sortDir`, `format` (csv|excel)

### 2. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.service.ts`
Single class `InventoryService` exported as singleton `inventoryService`. No Express types — pure business logic.

**Private helpers:**
- `generateRefNum(type: 'RCV'|'ISS'|'TRF'|'STC'|'ADJ'): string` → `RCV-20240301-A1B2`
- `buildLedgerWhere(query)` — shared filter builder for ledger and export (DRY)
- `applyStockCountAdjustments(tx, stockCountId, lines, approvedBy)` — shared by finalize (auto-approve) and approve

**Master data methods:**
- `listItems(query)` — paginated, supports search (name/sku/category contains), isActive filter
- `createItem(input)` — direct create
- `getItem(id)` — include stockLevels+lots; throw 404 if missing
- `updateItem(id, input)` — partial update
- `listCategories()` — distinct category values from active items
- `listVendors(isAdmin)` — omit `contactEncrypted` for non-admin
- `createVendor(input)` — encrypt `contact` → `contactEncrypted` via `encrypt()`
- `updateVendor(id, input)` — encrypt contact if provided
- `listLots(itemId)` — filter by itemId
- `listStockLevels(itemId?, locationId?)` — filtered, include item/location/lot
- `updateThreshold(id, input)` — update safetyThreshold on StockLevel

**Movement methods:**
- `receive(input, performedBy)`:
  1. Fetch item → check `isLotControlled`; throw 422 `LOT_REQUIRED` if missing lotNumber
  2. Upsert lot via `@@unique([itemId, lotNumber])`
  3. Upsert StockLevel via `@@unique([itemId, locationId, lotId])` with `onHand: { increment: quantity }`
  4. Create `RECEIVING` ledger entry; `toLocationId = locationId`

- `issue(input, performedBy)`:
  1. Fetch item → isLotControlled check → throw 422 `LOT_REQUIRED`
  2. Find StockLevel; throw 404 if missing
  3. If `onHand < quantity` → throw 422 `INSUFFICIENT_STOCK`
  4. Decrement StockLevel
  5. Create `ISSUE` ledger entry; `fromLocationId = locationId`

- `transfer(input, performedBy)` — **must use `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})`**:
  1. Fetch item → isLotControlled check
  2. Inside tx: find source StockLevel; throw 404/422 as needed
  3. Decrement source; upsert dest (same pattern as receive)
  4. Create single `TRANSFER` ledger entry with both `fromLocationId` and `toLocationId`

**Stock count methods:**
- `createStockCount(locationId, initiatedBy)`:
  1. Fetch all StockLevels for locationId
  2. `prisma.$transaction`: create StockCount (DRAFT) + StockCountLines (systemQty=onHand, countedQty=onHand, varianceQty=0)

- `updateLines(stockCountId, input)`:
  1. Verify status === DRAFT; throw 422 `INVALID_STATE` otherwise
  2. For each line: recalculate varianceQty, variancePct (null if systemQty=0), varianceUsd (null if no unitPrice)

- `finalizeStockCount(stockCountId)`:
  1. Verify status === DRAFT
  2. Aggregate: `overallVariancePct = abs(totalVarianceQty) / totalSystemQty * 100`, `totalVarianceUsd = sum(abs(line.varianceUsd))`
  3. `needsApproval = overallVariancePct > 5 || totalVarianceUsd > 250`
  4. If needsApproval: set `PENDING_APPROVAL`; else: call `applyStockCountAdjustments` + set `APPROVED`, `completedAt`

- `approveStockCount(stockCountId, approvedBy)` — MANAGER/ADMIN: verify `PENDING_APPROVAL`, apply adjustments in tx, set APPROVED
- `rejectStockCount(stockCountId)` — set REJECTED (no ledger entries, no stock changes)

**`applyStockCountAdjustments(tx, stockCountId, lines, approvedBy)`:**
- For each line where `varianceQty !== 0`: update `StockLevel.onHand += varianceQty` + create `STOCK_COUNT` ledger entry

**Query/export methods:**
- `getLowStock(locationId?)` — filter `onHand < max(safetyThreshold, avgDailyUsage * 7)`, include item/location/lot, return with computed `threshold` field
- `getLedger(query, isAdmin)` — paginated, sorted, includes `columns` metadata array; mask `unitCostUsd = null` for non-admin; omit `unitCostUsd` from columns for non-admin
- `exportLedger(query, isAdmin, username)` — no pagination, mask costs for non-admin, call `exportToCsv`/`exportToExcel` from `lib/exporter.ts`, return `{ data, format, filename }`

### 3. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.controller.ts`
Class `InventoryController` exported as singleton `inventoryController`. Arrow-function methods. Pattern: parse → call service → `res.json(successResponse(...))`.

Business error handling in every method:
```typescript
} catch (err: any) {
  if (err.statusCode) {
    res.status(err.statusCode).json(errorResponse(err.code, err.message));
    return;
  }
  next(err);
}
```

`exportLedger` controller does NOT use `res.json` — instead sets `Content-Type` + `Content-Disposition` headers and calls `res.send(result.data)`.

`isAdmin` computed as `req.user!.role === 'ADMIN'` in each relevant method.

---

## Files to Update

### 4. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.routes.ts` (complete replacement)
Wire controller methods. Critical ordering: static routes before dynamic ones.

```
GET  /items             → listItems
POST /items             → createItem         (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /items/categories  → listCategories     ← BEFORE /items/:id
GET  /items/:id         → getItem
PATCH /items/:id        → updateItem         (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /vendors           → listVendors
POST /vendors           → createVendor       (ADMIN, MANAGER)
PATCH /vendors/:id      → updateVendor       (ADMIN, MANAGER)
GET  /lots              → listLots
GET  /stock-levels      → listStockLevels
PATCH /stock-levels/:id/threshold → updateThreshold (ADMIN, MANAGER)
POST /receive           → receive            (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /issue             → issue              (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /transfer          → transfer           (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /stock-counts      → listStockCounts    (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts      → createStockCount   (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
PUT  /stock-counts/:id/lines     → updateLines       (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts/:id/finalize  → finalizeStockCount(ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts/:id/approve   → approveStockCount (ADMIN, MANAGER)
POST /stock-counts/:id/reject    → rejectStockCount  (ADMIN, MANAGER)
GET  /low-stock         → getLowStock
GET  /ledger/export     → exportLedger       (MANAGER, ADMIN) ← BEFORE /ledger
GET  /ledger            → getLedger          (MANAGER, ADMIN)
```

### 5. `backend/src/lib/scheduler.ts`
Replace placeholder `nightly-kpi-aggregation` job with real implementation:
- Count distinct userId from `rateLimitLog` yesterday → `dau`
- Sum ISSUE quantities yesterday / avg onHand → `conversionRate` (inventory turnover proxy)
- `prisma.kpiDaily.upsert` by `date`

Add new `nightly-avg-usage-update` job at `0 3 * * *`:
- `prisma.inventoryLedger.groupBy(['itemId', 'fromLocationId', 'lotId'])` for last 30 days of ISSUE movements
- `avgDailyUsage = sum / 30`
- `prisma.stockLevel.updateMany(...)` per group

Use dynamic `import('./prisma')` pattern (already used by cache-cleanup job — do not add static imports at top).

---

## New Test Files

### 6. `unit_tests/inventory.test.ts`
Pure logic tests — no database, no HTTP. Inline the pure formulas extracted from service methods:
- **Variance calculation** (5 tests): zero variance auto-approves; >5% triggers approval; USD >$250 triggers approval even at low pct; both within bounds → auto-approve; null unitPrice contributes $0
- **Low-stock threshold** (4 tests): safetyThreshold wins when higher; avgDailyUsage*7 wins when higher; exact-match is not low-stock; zero avgDailyUsage falls back to safetyThreshold
- **Transfer validation** (4 tests): rejects when requested > onHand; allows when equal; allows when less; zero stock rejects

### 7. `API_tests/inventory.test.ts`
Integration tests using `api` and `loginAs`/`demoUsers` from `./helpers`. Module-level `let` vars for captured IDs.

- **Happy path** (6 tests): GET items → GET vendors → GET locations → POST receive → verify stock increment → POST issue → verify decrement → POST transfer → verify source/dest; also tests 422 for insufficient stock
- **RBAC** (6 tests): guest blocked from receive/issue/transfer; unauthenticated gets 401; clerk blocked from approve; non-admin cannot see contactEncrypted in vendor list
- **Stock count workflow** (4 tests): create DRAFT → update lines → finalize auto-approve (zero variance) → finalize PENDING_APPROVAL (high variance); MANAGER can approve PENDING_APPROVAL count
- **Ledger export & cost visibility** (4 tests): non-admin gets unitCostUsd=null + columns omits field; admin sees unitCostUsd; CSV export returns correct Content-Type + Content-Disposition; Excel export returns spreadsheetml content-type

---

## Key Technical Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `prisma.$transaction(async tx => {...})` for transfer | Need conditional read before write — array form can't do this |
| `updateMany` for avgDailyUsage in scheduler | `lotId` is nullable; Prisma unique update is unreliable with nullable composite keys |
| `lotId ?? null` in upsert where clauses | Prisma requires explicit `null`, not `undefined`, for nullable composite unique fields |
| Business errors as `Object.assign(new Error(...), { statusCode, code })` | Caught in controller before `next(err)`, returns clean JSON without hitting global handler |
| `GET /items/categories` declared before `GET /items/:id` | Express router matches first; `categories` would otherwise be treated as `:id` |
| `GET /ledger/export` declared before `GET /ledger` | Same route ordering issue |
| Lot-controlled validation in service (not schema) | Requires DB fetch to know `isLotControlled`; Zod schemas keep lotNumber/lotId optional |
| `applyStockCountAdjustments` private helper | Reused by both finalize (auto-approve) and approve — eliminates duplication |
| Nightly job uses dynamic `import('./prisma')` | Matches existing cache-cleanup pattern in scheduler.ts |
| Export does not paginate | Export fetches all matching records (remove skip/take) |

---

## Verification

After implementation, verify with the running stack (`docker compose up -d`):

```bash
# Login as clerk
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"clerk","password":"clerk123!"}' | jq -r .data.token)

# Receive stock
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/inventory/receive \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"vendorId":1,"itemId":1,"locationId":1,"quantity":50,"unitCostUsd":9.99}'

# Issue stock
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/inventory/issue \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"itemId":1,"locationId":1,"quantity":5}'

# Create and finalize stock count
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/inventory/stock-counts \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"locationId":1}'

# Run full test suite
./run_tests.sh
```

Expected: all unit tests pass, all API tests pass, no TypeScript compilation errors in Docker build.
"}

- `ledgerQuerySchema` — all existing filters + `sortField`, `sortDir`, `format` (csv|excel)

### 2. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.service.ts`
Single class `InventoryService` exported as singleton `inventoryService`. No Express types — pure business logic.

**Private helpers:**
- `generateRefNum(type: 'RCV'|'ISS'|'TRF'|'STC'|'ADJ'): string` → `RCV-20240301-A1B2`
- `buildLedgerWhere(query)` — shared filter builder for ledger and export (DRY)
- `applyStockCountAdjustments(tx, stockCountId, lines, approvedBy)` — shared by finalize (auto-approve) and approve

**Master data methods:**
- `listItems(query)` — paginated, supports search (name/sku/category contains), isActive filter
- `createItem(input)` — direct create
- `getItem(id)` — include stockLevels+lots; throw 404 if missing
- `updateItem(id, input)` — partial update
- `listCategories()` — distinct category values from active items
- `listVendors(isAdmin)` — omit `contactEncrypted` for non-admin
- `createVendor(input)` — encrypt `contact` → `contactEncrypted` via `encrypt()`
- `updateVendor(id, input)` — encrypt contact if provided
- `listLots(itemId)` — filter by itemId
- `listStockLevels(itemId?, locationId?)` — filtered, include item/location/lot
- `updateThreshold(id, input)` — update safetyThreshold on StockLevel

**Movement methods:**
- `receive(input, performedBy)`:
  1. Fetch item → check `isLotControlled`; throw 422 `LOT_REQUIRED` if missing lotNumber
  2. Upsert lot via `@@unique([itemId, lotNumber])`
  3. Upsert StockLevel via `@@unique([itemId, locationId, lotId])` with `onHand: { increment: quantity }`
  4. Create `RECEIVING` ledger entry; `toLocationId = locationId`

- `issue(input, performedBy)`:
  1. Fetch item → isLotControlled check → throw 422 `LOT_REQUIRED`
  2. Find StockLevel; throw 404 if missing
  3. If `onHand < quantity` → throw 422 `INSUFFICIENT_STOCK`
  4. Decrement StockLevel
  5. Create `ISSUE` ledger entry; `fromLocationId = locationId`

- `transfer(input, performedBy)` — **must use `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})`**:
  1. Fetch item → isLotControlled check
  2. Inside tx: find source StockLevel; throw 404/422 as needed
  3. Decrement source; upsert dest (same pattern as receive)
  4. Create single `TRANSFER` ledger entry with both `fromLocationId` and `toLocationId`

**Stock count methods:**
- `createStockCount(locationId, initiatedBy)`:
  1. Fetch all StockLevels for locationId
  2. `prisma.$transaction`: create StockCount (DRAFT) + StockCountLines (systemQty=onHand, countedQty=onHand, varianceQty=0)

- `updateLines(stockCountId, input)`:
  1. Verify status === DRAFT; throw 422 `INVALID_STATE` otherwise
  2. For each line: recalculate varianceQty, variancePct (null if systemQty=0), varianceUsd (null if no unitPrice)

- `finalizeStockCount(stockCountId)`:
  1. Verify status === DRAFT
  2. Aggregate: `overallVariancePct = abs(totalVarianceQty) / totalSystemQty * 100`, `totalVarianceUsd = sum(abs(line.varianceUsd))`
  3. `needsApproval = overallVariancePct > 5 || totalVarianceUsd > 250`
  4. If needsApproval: set `PENDING_APPROVAL`; else: call `applyStockCountAdjustments` + set `APPROVED`, `completedAt`

- `approveStockCount(stockCountId, approvedBy)` — MANAGER/ADMIN: verify `PENDING_APPROVAL`, apply adjustments in tx, set APPROVED
- `rejectStockCount(stockCountId)` — set REJECTED (no ledger entries, no stock changes)

**`applyStockCountAdjustments(tx, stockCountId, lines, approvedBy)`:**
- For each line where `varianceQty !== 0`: update `StockLevel.onHand += varianceQty` + create `STOCK_COUNT` ledger entry

**Query/export methods:**
- `getLowStock(locationId?)` — filter `onHand < max(safetyThreshold, avgDailyUsage * 7)`, include item/location/lot, return with computed `threshold` field
- `getLedger(query, isAdmin)` — paginated, sorted, includes `columns` metadata array; mask `unitCostUsd = null` for non-admin; omit `unitCostUsd` from columns for non-admin
- `exportLedger(query, isAdmin, username)` — no pagination, mask costs for non-admin, call `exportToCsv`/`exportToExcel` from `lib/exporter.ts`, return `{ data, format, filename }`

### 3. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.controller.ts`
Class `InventoryController` exported as singleton `inventoryController`. Arrow-function methods. Pattern: parse → call service → `res.json(successResponse(...))`.

Business error handling in every method:
```typescript
} catch (err: any) {
  if (err.statusCode) {
    res.status(err.statusCode).json(errorResponse(err.code, err.message));
    return;
  }
  next(err);
}
```

`exportLedger` controller does NOT use `res.json` — instead sets `Content-Type` + `Content-Disposition` headers and calls `res.send(result.data)`.

`isAdmin` computed as `req.user!.role === 'ADMIN'` in each relevant method.

---

## Files to Update

### 4. `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.routes.ts` (complete replacement)
Wire controller methods. Critical ordering: static routes before dynamic ones.

```
GET  /items             → listItems
POST /items             → createItem         (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /items/categories  → listCategories     ← BEFORE /items/:id
GET  /items/:id         → getItem
PATCH /items/:id        → updateItem         (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /vendors           → listVendors
POST /vendors           → createVendor       (ADMIN, MANAGER)
PATCH /vendors/:id      → updateVendor       (ADMIN, MANAGER)
GET  /lots              → listLots
GET  /stock-levels      → listStockLevels
PATCH /stock-levels/:id/threshold → updateThreshold (ADMIN, MANAGER)
POST /receive           → receive            (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /issue             → issue              (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /transfer          → transfer           (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
GET  /stock-counts      → listStockCounts    (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts      → createStockCount   (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
PUT  /stock-counts/:id/lines     → updateLines       (ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts/:id/finalize  → finalizeStockCount(ADMIN, MANAGER, INVENTORY_CLERK)
POST /stock-counts/:id/approve   → approveStockCount (ADMIN, MANAGER)
POST /stock-counts/:id/reject    → rejectStockCount  (ADMIN, MANAGER)
GET  /low-stock         → getLowStock
GET  /ledger/export     → exportLedger       (MANAGER, ADMIN) ← BEFORE /ledger
GET  /ledger            → getLedger          (MANAGER, ADMIN)
```

### 5. `backend/src/lib/scheduler.ts`
Replace placeholder `nightly-kpi-aggregation` job with real implementation:
- Count distinct userId from `rateLimitLog` yesterday → `dau`
- Sum ISSUE quantities yesterday / avg onHand → `conversionRate` (inventory turnover proxy)
- `prisma.kpiDaily.upsert` by `date`

Add new `nightly-avg-usage-update` job at `0 3 * * *`:
- `prisma.inventoryLedger.groupBy(['itemId', 'fromLocationId', 'lotId'])` for last 30 days of ISSUE movements
- `avgDailyUsage = sum / 30`
- `prisma.stockLevel.updateMany(...)` per group

Use dynamic `import('./prisma')` pattern (already used by cache-cleanup job — do not add static imports at top).

---

## New Test Files

### 6. `unit_tests/inventory.test.ts`
Pure logic tests — no database, no HTTP. Inline the pure formulas extracted from service methods:
- **Variance calculation** (5 tests): zero variance auto-approves; >5% triggers approval; USD >$250 triggers approval even at low pct; both within bounds → auto-approve; null unitPrice contributes $0
- **Low-stock threshold** (4 tests): safetyThreshold wins when higher; avgDailyUsage*7 wins when higher; exact-match is not low-stock; zero avgDailyUsage falls back to safetyThreshold
- **Transfer validation** (4 tests): rejects when requested > onHand; allows when equal; allows when less; zero stock rejects

### 7. `API_tests/inventory.test.ts`
Integration tests using `api` and `loginAs`/`demoUsers` from `./helpers`. Module-level `let` vars for captured IDs.

- **Happy path** (6 tests): GET items → GET vendors → GET locations → POST receive → verify stock increment → POST issue → verify decrement → POST transfer → verify source/dest; also tests 422 for insufficient stock
- **RBAC** (6 tests): guest blocked from receive/issue/transfer; unauthenticated gets 401; clerk blocked from approve; non-admin cannot see contactEncrypted in vendor list
- **Stock count workflow** (4 tests): create DRAFT → update lines → finalize auto-approve (zero variance) → finalize PENDING_APPROVAL (high variance); MANAGER can approve PENDING_APPROVAL count
- **Ledger export & cost visibility** (4 tests): non-admin gets unitCostUsd=null + columns omits field; admin sees unitCostUsd; CSV export returns correct Content-Type + Content-Disposition; Excel export returns spreadsheetml content-type

---

## Key Technical Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `prisma.$transaction(async tx => {...})` for transfer | Need conditional read before write — array form can't do this |
| `updateMany` for avgDailyUsage in scheduler | `lotId` is nullable; Prisma unique update is unreliable with nullable composite keys |
| `lotId ?? null` in upsert where clauses | Prisma requires explicit `null`, not `undefined`, for nullable composite unique fields |
| Business errors as `Object.assign(new Error(...), { statusCode, code })` | Caught in controller before `next(err)`, returns clean JSON without hitting global handler |
| `GET /items/categories` declared before `GET /items/:id` | Express router matches first; `categories` would otherwise be treated as `:id` |
| `GET /ledger/export` declared before `GET /ledger` | Same route ordering issue |
| Lot-controlled validation in service (not schema) | Requires DB fetch to know `isLotControlled`; Zod schemas keep lotNumber/lotId optional |
| `applyStockCountAdjustments` private helper | Reused by both finalize (auto-approve) and approve — eliminates duplication |
| Nightly job uses dynamic `import('./prisma')` | Matches existing cache-cleanup pattern in scheduler.ts |
| Export does not paginate | Export fetches all matching records (remove skip/take) |

---

```bash
# Receive stock
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/inventory/receive \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"vendorId":1,"itemId":1,"locationId":1,"quantity":50,"unitCostUsd":9.99}'

# Issue stock
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/inventory/issue \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"itemId":1,"locationId":1,"quantity":5}'

# Create and finalize stock count

  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"locationId":1}'

# Run full test suite
./run_tests.sh
```

content.text:
The implementation is complete. Here's a summary of everything done:

---

## Summary

### Files Created
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.schema.ts` | Zod validators for all endpoints (items, vendors, movements, stock counts, ledger queries) |
| `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.service.ts` | Full business logic — receiving, issue, transfer, stock counts, low-stock, ledger, exports, KPI |
| `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.controller.ts` | Thin HTTP handlers — parse → service → respond |
| `unit_tests/inventory.test.ts` | 20 pure-logic unit tests (variance calculation, low-stock threshold, transfer validation, ref numbers) |
| `API_tests/inventory.test.ts` | 25 integration tests across master data, movements, RBAC, stock counts, ledger exports |

### Files Updated
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.routes.ts` | Complete replacement — wires controller, correct static-before-dynamic ordering |
| `backend/src/lib/scheduler.ts` | Real KPI aggregation (DAU + inventory turnover) + new avg-usage-update job |

### What's Now Working
- **Receiving**: vendor + item + lot upsert + stock level increment + immutable RECEIVING ledger entry
- **Issue**: lot-controlled check + negative-stock guard + decrement + ISSUE ledger entry  
- **Transfer**: `$transaction`-wrapped atomic decrement source / upsert dest + TRANSFER ledger entry with both locationIds; rejects same-source-dest and insufficient stock
- **Stock counts**: full DRAFT → finalize → (auto-APPROVED or PENDING_APPROVAL) → manager approve/reject workflow; adjustments written as STOCK_COUNT ledger entries
- **Low-stock**: threshold = `max(safetyThreshold, avgDailyUsage * 7)` with computed `threshold` field in response
- **Ledger**: paginated + sorted + full filter set; `unitCostUsd` masked null + omitted from `columns` for non-admin
- **Exports**: CSV and Excel via `lib/exporter.ts` with username/timestamp watermark; correct `Content-Disposition` headers; cost masking for non-admin
- **KPI cron** (2 AM): DAU from rate_limit_logs + inventory turnover → `kpi_daily` upsert
- **Avg-usage cron** (3 AM): 30-day ISSUE aggregation → `stockLevel.avgDailyUsage` updates
- **RBAC**: all 23 routes enforce correct role requirements; contact encryption visible only to ADMIN

### Risks / Assumptions
- `receive` stock-level find-or-create is not inside a transaction (acceptable for single-tenant load; race condition on concurrent receives for identical item/location/lot would require a unique constraint retry)
- Stock count `systemQty` is snapshotted at creation time — concurrent receiving during a stock count session is expected behavior and reconciles correctly at finalize
- Existing `smoke.test.ts` and `rbac.test.ts` tests remain compatible — all previously tested endpoints are still present at the same paths

You are acting as a senior production engineer and staff-level full-stack architect working inside an existing repository.

## Core operating rules
- First inspect the current repository structure before making changes.
- Preserve and extend the existing architecture unless it is clearly incorrect or blocks requirements.
- Prefer updating existing files over creating redundant alternatives.
- Keep the codebase clean, modular, and production-grade.
- Do not generate pseudo-code.
- Do not leave TODOs or placeholders for core requirements.
- Do not weaken or simplify explicit requirements from the task prompt.
- Do not replace required workflows with mocks, fake logic, or static hardcoded responses.
- Keep controllers thin and move business logic into services.
- Use clear naming and semantic directory structure.
- Validate all API inputs (body/query/path) and return clean JSON errors.
- Use structured logging for important business events.
- Preserve one-command startup: `docker compose up`
- Do not require manual environment file creation or manual DB setup.
- Use only public dependencies and public base images.
- Do not rely on the host machine or global tools.
- After implementation, always summarize:
  1. files created/updated
  2. what is now working
  3. any remaining risks or assumptions

Continue from the existing HarborOps repository. Inspect the current codebase first, then extend it cleanly.

# Scope for THIS step
Fully implement these backend domains end-to-end in the existing Express + TypeScript + Prisma + MySQL codebase:

## 1. Guest post-stay reviews
Implement:
- users submit 1–5 ratings for:
  - cleanliness
  - communication
  - accuracy
- text review
- predefined tags
- up to 6 images
- each image max 5 MB
- follow-up review allowed within 7 days
- host can reply ONCE within 14 days

## 2. Two-way rating and trust / credit system for service interactions
Implement:
- service interaction records
- post-task two-way reviews
- trust/credit impact calculation
- clear stored explanation of why credit changed
- immutable credit history
- API returns “credit impact explanation” for UI display

## 3. Governance / moderation
Implement:
- local sensitive-word filtering using a locally maintained dictionary
- anti-spam rate limiting default 3 reviews per user per hour
- reported content queue for Moderator triage
- moderation actions with audit trail
- moderation status changes
- restricted moderation audit access

## 4. Appeals workflow
Implement:
- appeals for relevant trust/review moderation outcomes
- statuses
- arbitration notes
- final outcomes
- audit persistence
- clean state transitions

## 5. Promotion engine
Implement a real pricing and promotion engine with:
- effective dates
- mutual exclusions
- priority rules
- deterministic conflict resolution:
  - evaluate highest priority first
  - if priorities tie, choose maximum savings
- preserve traceable “why this discount applied” history
- checkout pricing endpoint returns:
  - best offer
  - itemized discount breakdown
  - explanation history

## 6. Product search / merchandising
Implement:
- full-text keyword search using MySQL full-text indexes
- category filters
- attribute filters
- sorting
- suggested terms
- trending keywords visible to managers
- local cache with TTL
- pagination

## 7. Security requirements
Implement and/or enforce:
- sensitive fields like phone numbers masked in UI-facing responses
- encrypted at rest for sensitive fields (at minimum phone numbers)
- RBAC restrictions preserved
- moderation audit trails restricted to authorized roles
- non-admin restrictions on credit-rule configuration

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

**Step 1 (Previous Session - Compacted):** Build complete HarborOps production-grade full-stack lodging & supply management system with Docker Compose one-command startup, real authentication, 35-model Prisma schema, RBAC, and comprehensive test infrastructure.

**Step 2 (Inventory Module):** Fully implement backend inventory domains: master data (items/vendors/locations/lots), procurement receiving, stock issue, stock transfer (no-negative guard), stock count cycle counts with variance approval workflow, low-stock alerts, immutable ledger query center, CSV/Excel exports with watermarks, and KPI nightly aggregation cron jobs.

**Step 3 (Current - Partially Complete):** Fully implement: guest post-stay reviews (1-5 ratings, text, tags, up to 6 images, follow-up within 7 days, host reply within 14 days), two-way trust/credit system, governance/moderation (sensitive-word filter, report queue, actions, audit trail), appeals workflow, promotion engine (priority-based conflict resolution, max-savings tiebreaker), product search (MySQL full-text indexes, attribute filters, caching, suggestions, trending), and security requirements.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Express + TypeScript + Prisma ORM + MySQL 8 backend architecture
   - Domain modules: schema.ts / service.ts / controller.ts / routes.ts layering
   - Prisma 5.22 interactive transactions `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})`
   - `any` type for dynamic Prisma where clauses (strict types reject `Record<string, unknown>`)
   - `businessError(code, message, statusCode)` pattern (throws with attached properties)
   - Immutable inventory ledger (RECEIVING, ISSUE, TRANSFER, STOCK_COUNT, ADJUSTMENT)
   - Stock count variance approval: >5% OR >$250 triggers PENDING_APPROVAL
   - AES-256-GCM encryption for sensitive fields (phone, vendor contact)
   - JWT + bcrypt with per-user salt authentication
   - RBAC via `authorize(...roles)` middleware
   - TTL in-memory cache (`lib/cache.ts`) with 15-minute default
   - Multer disk storage for image uploads (UUID filenames, 5MB limit, 6 max)
   - Database-backed rate limiter (RateLimitLog table, 3 reviews/hour default)
   - `lib/contentFilter.ts` - loads sensitive words from DB (30-min cache), normalizes and scans text
   - node-cron scheduled jobs (2 AM KPI aggregation, 3 AM avg-usage update, 30-min cache cleanup)
   - CSV/Excel exports via `lib/exporter.ts` with username/timestamp watermarks
   - MySQL full-text indexes (`@@fulltext([name, description, sku])` on Item)
   - Trust credit system: 5★→+2, 4★→+1, 3★→0, 2★→-1, 1★→-2, score clamped 0-100
   - Promotion engine: highest priority first, max savings tiebreaker, mutual exclusions
   - Review timing rules: follow-up ≤7 days, host reply ≤14 days
   - Vitest + Supertest test infrastructure (unit_tests/ and API_tests/)
   - Docker Compose multi-service orchestration (mysql:8, backend, frontend)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   **`backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.schema.ts`** (created)
   - All Zod validators for inventory operations
   - Key schemas: `receiveSchema`, `issueSchema`, `transferSchema`, `createStockCountSchema`, `updateLinesSchema`, `ledgerQuerySchema` (with sortField/sortDir/format)

   **`backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.service.ts`** (created)
   - Uses `any` for `tx` in `applyStockCountAdjustments` (avoids Prisma v5 tx typing complexity)
   - Uses `any` for all dynamic `where` clauses (Prisma strict types reject Record<string,unknown>)
   - `buildLedgerWhere()` private method shared between `getLedger` and `exportLedger`
   - `applyStockCountAdjustments(tx: any, stockCountId, locationId, initiatedBy, lines, approvedBy)` - private helper used by both auto-approve in `finalizeStockCount` and explicit `approveStockCount`
   - `receive()` - uses `findFirst` + create/update instead of `upsert` for reliable null lotId handling
   - `transfer()` - uses `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})` (interactive form, not array form)
   - `getLedger()` returns `columns` metadata array; unitCostUsd masked null for non-admin
   - `exportLedger()` returns `{ data, format, filename }` with flat data for exporter
   - `getLowStock()` returns items with computed `threshold` and `isLowStock` fields

   ```typescript
   // Key businessError pattern:
   function businessError(code: string, message: string, statusCode: number): never {
     throw Object.assign(new Error(message), { statusCode, code });
   }
   
   // Key transfer transaction:
   const ledgerEntry = await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
     const source = await tx.stockLevel.findFirst({ where: { itemId, locationId: fromLocationId, lotId: lotId ?? null } });
     if (!source) businessError('NOT_FOUND', 'Source stock level not found', 404);
     if (source!.onHand < input.quantity) businessError('INSUFFICIENT_STOCK', '...', 422);
     await tx.stockLevel.update({ where: { id: source!.id }, data: { onHand: { decrement: input.quantity } } });
     // upsert dest...
     return tx.inventoryLedger.create({ data: { movementType: 'TRANSFER', fromLocationId, toLocationId, ... } });
   });
   ```

   **`backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.controller.ts`** (created)
   - Arrow function methods on `InventoryController` class
   - Catch pattern: `if (err.statusCode) { res.status(err.statusCode).json(errorResponse(err.code, err.message)); return; }`
   - `exportLedger` sets Content-Type + Content-Disposition headers, calls `res.send()` not `res.json()`
   - `isAdmin = req.user!.role === 'ADMIN'` computed in each relevant method

   **`backend/src/modules/inventory/inventory.routes.ts`** (replaced entirely)
   - Critical: `GET /items/categories` BEFORE `GET /items/:id`
   - Critical: `GET /ledger/export` BEFORE `GET /ledger`
   - Uses `req as any` cast for AuthenticatedRequest compatibility

   **`backend/src/lib/scheduler.ts`** (updated)
   - Replaced placeholder nightly KPI job:
     ```typescript
     registerJob('nightly-kpi-aggregation', '0 2 * * *', async () => {
       // DAU from rateLimitLog.groupBy(['userId'])
       // conversionRate = totalIssues / avgStock
       // prisma.kpiDaily.upsert({ where: { date: yesterday }, ... })
     });
     ```
   - Added new job:
     ```typescript
     registerJob('nightly-avg-usage-update', '0 3 * * *', async () => {
       // groupBy(['itemId', 'fromLocationId', 'lotId']) on last 30 days ISSUE movements
       // avgDailyUsage = sum / 30
       // stockLevel.updateMany(...)
     });
     ```
   - Both use dynamic `import('./prisma')` pattern (matches cache-cleanup pattern)

   **`unit_tests/inventory.test.ts`** (created)
   - 20 pure-logic tests, no DB/HTTP
   - Variance calculation tests (inline formula), low-stock threshold, transfer validation, reference number format

   **`API_tests/inventory.test.ts`** (created)
   - 25 integration tests using `api` and `loginAs`/`demoUsers` from helpers
   - Tests: master data, receive→issue→transfer happy path, RBAC, stock count workflow, ledger exports
   - Verifies unitCostUsd is null for manager but present for admin
   - Verifies CSV has `text/csv` Content-Type and `attachment` Content-Disposition

   **`backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.schema.ts`** (created - Step 3)
   ```typescript
   createReviewSchema: { revieweeId?, targetType, targetId, ratingCleanliness, ratingCommunication, ratingAccuracy (all 1-5), text?, tagIds? }
   createFollowUpSchema: same ratings + text + tagIds

   ```

   **`backend/src/modules/trust/trust.schema.ts`** (created - Step 3)


   **`backend/src/modules/moderation/moderation.schema.ts`** (created - Step 3)
   ```typescript
   fileReportSchema: { contentType, contentId, reviewId?, reason (min 10) }
   takeActionSchema: { action: WARN|HIDE|REMOVE|RESTORE, notes? }
   fileAppealSchema: { moderationActionId, userStatement (min 10) }

   addSensitiveWordSchema: { word, category? }
   ```

   **`backend/src/modules/promotions/promotions.schema.ts`** (created - Step 3)

   ```typescript
   updatePromotionSchema: all partial
   checkoutSchema: { items: [{ itemId, quantity }] min 1 }
   ```

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **`Record<string, unknown>` vs Prisma types:** Prisma's strict TypeScript types rejected `Record<string, unknown>` for where clauses. Fixed by using `any` type for all dynamic where clause variables, with eslint-disable comments.
   - **`PrismaTx` type for interactive transactions:** Initially tried `Omit<PrismaClient, '$connect'|'$disconnect'|'$on'|'$transaction'|'$use'|'$extends'>` but Prisma v5 removed `$use`, and overloaded `$transaction` type extraction via `Parameters<>` is unreliable. Fixed by changing `applyStockCountAdjustments` parameter to `tx: any`.
   - **Dynamic `orderBy` key:** `{ [query.sortField]: query.sortDir }` rejected by Prisma strict types. Fixed with `as any` cast.
   - **Misplaced type alias:** `type PrismaTx` was accidentally placed between import statements. Fixed by reorganizing imports to all be at top, then type alias after imports.
   - **Unused `MovementType` import:** Was imported from `@prisma/client` for type cast in `buildLedgerWhere`. Removed cast (since `where` is `any`), removed import.
   - **`updateVendor` data parameter:** `Record<string, unknown>` rejected by Prisma for `data` param. Fixed with `const data: any = {}`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Stock level null-lotId upsert:** Prisma composite unique `[itemId, locationId, lotId]` with nullable `lotId` has unreliable behavior with Prisma's `upsert`. Solved with explicit `findFirst` + `create`/`update` pattern instead.
   - **Transfer atomicity:** Used `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})` interactive form (not array form) because we need to conditionally read before writing.
   - **Ledger export column metadata:** Returns `columns` array with/without `unitCostUsd` based on isAdmin, allowing frontend to dynamically show/hide columns.
   - **Route ordering for static vs dynamic paths:** Express matches routes in declaration order; `GET /items/categories` must appear before `GET /items/:id`, and `GET /ledger/export` before `GET /ledger`.

content.text:
1	generator client {
2	  provider        = "prisma-client-js"
3	  previewFeatures = ["fullTextSearch", "fullTextIndex"]
4	  binaryTargets   = ["native", "linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x"]
5	}
6	
7	datasource db {
8	  provider = "mysql"
9	  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
10	}
11	
12	// ─── AUTH & USERS ───────────────────────────────────────────────
13	
14	enum Role {
15	  ADMIN
16	  MANAGER
17	  INVENTORY_CLERK
18	  FRONT_DESK
19	  HOST
20	  GUEST
21	  MODERATOR
22	}
23	
24	model User {
25	  id              Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
26	  username        String   @unique @db.VarChar(100)
27	  passwordHash    String   @map("password_hash") @db.VarChar(255)
28	  salt            String   @db.VarChar(64)
29	  role            Role     @default(GUEST)
30	  displayName     String   @map("display_name") @db.VarChar(200)
31	  phoneEncrypted  String?  @map("phone_encrypted") @db.VarChar(500)
32	  phoneMasked     String?  @map("phone_masked") @db.VarChar(20)
33	  isActive        Boolean  @default(true) @map("is_active")
34	  createdAt       DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
35	  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
36	
37	  // Relations
38	  ledgerEntries       InventoryLedger[]   @relation("performer")
39	  approvedLedgers     InventoryLedger[]   @relation("approver")
40	  stockCountsInitiated StockCount[]       @relation("initiator")
41	  stockCountsApproved  StockCount[]       @relation("stockApprover")
42	  reviewsWritten      Review[]            @relation("reviewer")
43	  reviewsReceived     Review[]            @relation("reviewee")
44	  hostReplies         HostReply[]
45	  trustScore          TrustScore?
46	  creditHistory       CreditHistory[]
47	  reportsSubmitted    Report[]            @relation("reporter")
48	  reportsAssigned     Report[]            @relation("assignee")
49	  moderationActions   ModerationAction[]
50	  appeals             Appeal[]
51	  searchLogs          SearchLog[]
52	  scheduledReports    ScheduledReport[]
53	  rateLimitLogs       RateLimitLog[]
54	  taskRatingsGiven    TaskRating[]        @relation("taskRater")
55	  taskRatingsReceived TaskRating[]        @relation("taskRatee")
56	
57	  @@map("users")
58	}
59	
60	// ─── LOCATIONS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
61	
62	model Location {
63	  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
64	  name      String   @unique @db.VarChar(200)
65	  address   String?  @db.VarChar(500)
66	  isActive  Boolean  @default(true) @map("is_active")
67	  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
68	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
69	
70	  stockLevels     StockLevel[]
71	  ledgerFrom      InventoryLedger[] @relation("fromLocation")
72	  ledgerTo        InventoryLedger[] @relation("toLocation")
73	  stockCounts     StockCount[]
74	
75	  @@map("locations")
76	}
77	
78	// ─── INVENTORY ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
79	
80	model Item {
81	  id              Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
82	  name            String   @db.VarChar(200)
83	  sku             String   @unique @db.VarChar(100)
84	  category        String   @db.VarChar(100)
85	  description     String?  @db.Text
86	  isLotControlled Boolean  @default(false) @map("is_lot_controlled")
87	  unitOfMeasure   String   @default("EA") @map("unit_of_measure") @db.VarChar(20)
88	  unitPrice       Decimal? @map("unit_price") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
89	  isActive        Boolean  @default(true) @map("is_active")
90	  createdAt       DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
91	  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
92	
93	  lots              Lot[]
94	  stockLevels       StockLevel[]
95	  ledgerEntries     InventoryLedger[]
96	  stockCountLines   StockCountLine[]
97	  promotionItems    PromotionItem[]
98	  productAttributes ProductAttribute[]
99	  appliedPromotions AppliedPromotion[]
100	
101	  @@fulltext([name, description, sku])
102	  @@map("items")
103	}
104	
105	model Vendor {
106	  id                Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
107	  name              String   @unique @db.VarChar(200)
108	  contactEncrypted  String?  @map("contact_encrypted") @db.VarChar(500)
109	  isActive          Boolean  @default(true) @map("is_active")
110	  createdAt         DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
111	  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
112	
113	  ledgerEntries InventoryLedger[]
114	
115	  @@map("vendors")
116	}
117	
118	model Lot {
119	  id             Int       @id @default(autoincrement())
120	  itemId         Int       @map("item_id")
121	  lotNumber      String    @map("lot_number") @db.VarChar(100)
122	  expirationDate DateTime? @map("expiration_date")
123	  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now()) @map("created_at")
124	
125	  item          Item              @relation(fields: [itemId], references: [id])
126	  stockLevels   StockLevel[]
127	  ledgerEntries InventoryLedger[]
128	  stockCountLines StockCountLine[]
129	
130	  @@unique([itemId, lotNumber])
131	  @@map("lots")
132	}
133	
134	model StockLevel {
135	  id              Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
136	  itemId          Int      @map("item_id")
137	  locationId      Int      @map("location_id")
138	  lotId           Int?     @map("lot_id")
139	  onHand          Int      @default(0) @map("on_hand")
140	  safetyThreshold Int      @default(10) @map("safety_threshold")
141	  avgDailyUsage   Decimal  @default(0) @map("avg_daily_usage") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
142	  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
143	
144	  item     Item     @relation(fields: [itemId], references: [id])
145	  location Location @relation(fields: [locationId], references: [id])
146	  lot      Lot?     @relation(fields: [lotId], references: [id])
147	
148	  @@unique([itemId, locationId, lotId])
149	  @@map("stock_levels")
150	}
151	
152	// ─── INVENTORY LEDGER (immutable) ───────────────────────────────
153	
154	enum MovementType {
155	  RECEIVING
156	  ISSUE
157	  TRANSFER
158	  STOCK_COUNT
159	  ADJUSTMENT
160	}
161	
162	model InventoryLedger {
163	  id               Int          @id @default(autoincrement())
164	  itemId           Int          @map("item_id")
165	  lotId            Int?         @map("lot_id")
166	  fromLocationId   Int?         @map("from_location_id")
167	  toLocationId     Int?         @map("to_location_id")
168	  movementType     MovementType @map("movement_type")
169	  quantity         Int
170	  unitCostUsd      Decimal?     @map("unit_cost_usd") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
171	  vendorId         Int?         @map("vendor_id")
172	  packSize         Int?         @map("pack_size")
173	  deliveryDatetime DateTime?    @map("delivery_datetime")
174	  referenceNumber  String       @map("reference_number") @db.VarChar(100)
175	  performedBy      Int          @map("performed_by")
176	  approvedBy       Int?         @map("approved_by")
177	  notes            String?      @db.Text
178	  createdAt        DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")
179	
180	  item         Item      @relation(fields: [itemId], references: [id])
181	  lot          Lot?      @relation(fields: [lotId], references: [id])
182	  fromLocation Location? @relation("fromLocation", fields: [fromLocationId], references: [id])
183	  toLocation   Location? @relation("toLocation", fields: [toLocationId], references: [id])
184	  vendor       Vendor?   @relation(fields: [vendorId], references: [id])
185	  performer    User      @relation("performer", fields: [performedBy], references: [id])
186	  approver     User?     @relation("approver", fields: [approvedBy], references: [id])
187	
188	  @@index([itemId, createdAt])
189	  @@index([fromLocationId, movementType, createdAt])
190	  @@index([toLocationId, movementType, createdAt])
191	  @@index([vendorId, createdAt])
192	  @@index([lotId])
193	  @@map("inventory_ledger")
194	}
195	
196	// ─── STOCK COUNTS ───────────────────────────────────────────────
197	
198	enum StockCountStatus {
199	  DRAFT
200	  PENDING_APPROVAL
201	  APPROVED
202	  REJECTED
203	}
204	
205	model StockCount {
206	  id          Int              @id @default(autoincrement())
207	  locationId  Int              @map("location_id")
208	  initiatedBy Int              @map("initiated_by")
209	  status      StockCountStatus @default(DRAFT)
210	  variancePct Decimal?         @map("variance_pct") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
211	  varianceUsd Decimal?         @map("variance_usd") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
212	  approvedBy  Int?             @map("approved_by")
213	  createdAt   DateTime         @default(now()) @map("created_at")
214	  completedAt DateTime?        @map("completed_at")
215	
216	  location  Location         @relation(fields: [locationId], references: [id])
217	  initiator User             @relation("initiator", fields: [initiatedBy], references: [id])
218	  approver  User?            @relation("stockApprover", fields: [approvedBy], references: [id])
219	  lines     StockCountLine[]
220	
221	  @@map("stock_counts")
222	}
223	
224	model StockCountLine {
225	  id           Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
226	  stockCountId Int      @map("stock_count_id")
227	  itemId       Int      @map("item_id")
228	  lotId        Int?     @map("lot_id")
229	  systemQty    Int      @map("system_qty")
230	  countedQty   Int      @map("counted_qty")
231	  varianceQty  Int      @map("variance_qty")
232	  variancePct  Decimal? @map("variance_pct") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
233	  varianceUsd  Decimal? @map("variance_usd") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
234	
235	  stockCount StockCount @relation(fields: [stockCountId], references: [id])
236	  item       Item       @relation(fields: [itemId], references: [id])
237	  lot        Lot?       @relation(fields: [lotId], references: [id])
238	
239	  @@map("stock_count_lines")
240	}
241	
242	// ─── REVIEWS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
243	
244	enum ReviewStatus {
245	  ACTIVE
246	  FLAGGED
247	  HIDDEN
248	  REMOVED
249	}
250	
251	enum ReviewTargetType {
252	  STAY
253	  TASK
254	}
255	
256	model Review {
257	  id                   Int              @id @default(autoincrement())
258	  reviewerId           Int              @map("reviewer_id")
259	  revieweeId           Int?             @map("reviewee_id")
260	  targetType           ReviewTargetType @map("target_type")
261	  targetId             Int              @map("target_id")
262	  ratingCleanliness    Int              @map("rating_cleanliness") @db.TinyInt
263	  ratingCommunication  Int              @map("rating_communication") @db.TinyInt
264	  ratingAccuracy       Int              @map("rating_accuracy") @db.TinyInt
265	  overallRating        Decimal          @map("overall_rating") @db.Decimal(2, 1)
266	  text                 String?          @db.Text
267	  isFollowUp           Boolean          @default(false) @map("is_follow_up")
268	  parentReviewId       Int?             @map("parent_review_id")
269	  status               ReviewStatus     @default(ACTIVE)
270	  createdAt            DateTime         @default(now()) @map("created_at")
271	
272	  reviewer      User          @relation("reviewer", fields: [reviewerId], references: [id])
273	  reviewee      User?         @relation("reviewee", fields: [revieweeId], references: [id])
274	  parentReview  Review?       @relation("followUps", fields: [parentReviewId], references: [id])
275	  followUps     Review[]      @relation("followUps")
276	  images        ReviewImage[]
277	  tags          ReviewTag[]
278	  hostReply     HostReply?
279	  reports       Report[]
280	
281	  @@fulltext([text])
282	  @@map("reviews")
283	}
284	
285	model ReviewImage {
286	  id         Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
287	  reviewId   Int      @map("review_id")
288	  filePath   String   @map("file_path") @db.VarChar(500)
289	  fileSize   Int      @map("file_size")
290	  uploadedAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("uploaded_at")
291	
292	  review Review @relation(fields: [reviewId], references: [id])
293	
294	  @@map("review_images")
295	}
296	
297	model Tag {
298	  id       Int         @id @default(autoincrement())
299	  name     String      @unique @db.VarChar(100)
300	  category String?     @db.VarChar(100)
301	
302	  reviews ReviewTag[]
303	
304	  @@map("tags")
305	}
306	
307	model ReviewTag {
308	  id       Int @id @default(autoincrement())
309	  reviewId Int @map("review_id")
310	  tagId    Int @map("tag_id")
311	
312	  review Review @relation(fields: [reviewId], references: [id])
313	  tag    Tag    @relation(fields: [tagId], references: [id])
314	
315	  @@unique([reviewId, tagId])
316	  @@map("review_tags")
317	}
318	
319	model HostReply {
320	  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
321	  reviewId  Int      @unique @map("review_id")
322	  hostId    Int      @map("host_id")
323	  text      String   @db.Text
324	  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
325	
326	  review Review @relation(fields: [reviewId], references: [id])
327	  host   User   @relation(fields: [hostId], references: [id])
328	
329	  @@map("host_replies")
330	}
331	
332	// ─── TRUST & CREDIT ─────────────────────────────────────────────
333	
334	model TrustScore {
335	  id          Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
336	  userId      Int      @unique @map("user_id")
337	  score       Decimal  @default(50.00) @db.Decimal(5, 2)
338	  lastUpdated DateTime @default(now()) @map("last_updated")
339	
340	  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
341	
342	  @@map("trust_scores")
343	}
344	
345	model CreditHistory {
346	  id           Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
347	  userId       Int      @map("user_id")
348	  changeAmount Decimal  @map("change_amount") @db.Decimal(5, 2)
349	  reason       String   @db.VarChar(500)
350	  sourceType   String   @map("source_type") @db.VarChar(50)
351	  sourceId     Int      @map("source_id")
352	  explanation  String   @db.Text
353	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
354	
355	  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
356	
357	  @@index([userId, createdAt])
358	  @@map("credit_history")
359	}
360	
361	model TaskRating {
362	  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
363	  raterId   Int      @map("rater_id")
364	  rateeId   Int      @map("ratee_id")
365	  taskId    Int      @map("task_id")
366	  rating    Int      @db.TinyInt
367	  comment   String?  @db.Text
368	  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
369	
370	  rater User @relation("taskRater", fields: [raterId], references: [id])
371	  ratee User @relation("taskRatee", fields: [rateeId], references: [id])
372	
373	  @@unique([raterId, taskId])
374	  @@map("task_ratings")
375	}
376	
377	// ─── MODERATION ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
378	
379	enum ReportStatus {
380	  PENDING
381	  IN_REVIEW
382	  RESOLVED
383	  DISMISSED
384	}
385	
386	enum ModerationActionType {
387	  WARN
388	  HIDE
389	  REMOVE
390	  RESTORE
391	}
392	
393	model Report {
394	  id          Int          @id @default(autoincrement())
395	  reporterId  Int          @map("reporter_id")
396	  contentType String       @map("content_type") @db.VarChar(50)
397	  contentId   Int          @map("content_id")
398	  reviewId    Int?         @map("review_id")
399	  reason      String       @db.Text
400	  status      ReportStatus @default(PENDING)
401	  assignedTo  Int?         @map("assigned_to")
402	  createdAt   DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")
403	  resolvedAt  DateTime?    @map("resolved_at")
404	
405	  reporter User              @relation("reporter", fields: [reporterId], references: [id])
406	  assignee User?             @relation("assignee", fields: [assignedTo], references: [id])
407	  review   Review?           @relation(fields: [reviewId], references: [id])
408	  actions  ModerationAction[]
409	
410	  @@map("reports")
411	}
412	
413	model ModerationAction {
414	  id          Int                  @id @default(autoincrement())
415	  reportId    Int                  @map("report_id")
416	  moderatorId Int                  @map("moderator_id")
417	  action      ModerationActionType
418	  notes       String?              @db.Text
419	  createdAt   DateTime             @default(now()) @map("created_at")
420	
421	  report    Report   @relation(fields: [reportId], references: [id])
422	  moderator User     @relation(fields: [moderatorId], references: [id])
423	  appeals   Appeal[]
424	
425	  @@map("moderation_actions")
426	}
427	
428	enum AppealStatus {
429	  PENDING
430	  IN_REVIEW
431	  UPHELD
432	  OVERTURNED
433	}
434	
435	model Appeal {
436	  id                 Int          @id @default(autoincrement())
437	  userId             Int          @map("user_id")
438	  moderationActionId Int          @map("moderation_action_id")
439	  status             AppealStatus @default(PENDING)
440	  userStatement      String       @map("user_statement") @db.Text
441	  arbitrationNotes   String?      @map("arbitration_notes") @db.Text
442	  outcome            String?      @db.Text
443	  createdAt          DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")
444	  resolvedAt         DateTime?    @map("resolved_at")
445	
446	  user             User             @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
447	  moderationAction ModerationAction @relation(fields: [moderationActionId], references: [id])
448	
449	  @@map("appeals")
450	}
451	
452	model SensitiveWord {
453	  id       Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
454	  word     String   @unique @db.VarChar(200)
455	  category String?  @db.VarChar(100)

457

462	
463	enum DiscountType {
464	  PERCENTAGE
465	  FIXED_AMOUNT
466	}
467	
468	model Promotion {
469	  id             Int          @id @default(autoincrement())
470	  name           String       @db.VarChar(200)
471	  description    String?      @db.Text
472	  discountType   DiscountType @map("discount_type")

474	  effectiveStart DateTime     @map("effective_start")

476	  priority       Int          @default(0)
477	  isActive       Boolean      @default(true) @map("is_active")
478	  conditions     Json?
479	  createdAt      DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")
480	  updatedAt      DateTime     @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
481	
482	  exclusionsFrom PromotionExclusion[] @relation("excluder")
483	  exclusionsTo   PromotionExclusion[] @relation("excluded")
484	  items          PromotionItem[]
485	  applications   AppliedPromotion[]
486	
487	  @@index([isActive, priority, effectiveStart, effectiveEnd])
488	  @@map("promotions")
489	}
490	
491	model PromotionExclusion {

493	  promotionId         Int @map("promotion_id")
494	  excludedPromotionId Int @map("excluded_promotion_id")
495	
496	  promotion         Promotion @relation("excluder", fields: [promotionId], references: [id])
497	  excludedPromotion Promotion @relation("excluded", fields: [excludedPromotionId], references: [id])
498	
499	  @@unique([promotionId, excludedPromotionId])
500	  @@map("promotion_exclusions")
501	}
502	
503	model PromotionItem {

507	
508	  promotion Promotion @relation(fields: [promotionId], references: [id])
509	  item      Item      @relation(fields: [itemId], references: [id])
510	
511	  @@unique([promotionId, itemId])
512	  @@map("promotion_items")
513	}
514	
515	model AppliedPromotion {

524	
525	  item      Item      @relation(fields: [itemId], references: [id])
526	  promotion Promotion @relation(fields: [promotionId], references: [id])
527	
528	  @@map("applied_promotions")
529	}

540
